title: Andhra Style Drumstick Sambar
description: Rich and aromatic Andhra-style sambar with tender drumsticks, featuring tamarind tang, fresh ginger aroma, and aromatic tadka. Perfect with rice, dosa, idli, and vada.

subSteps: Place 18 g tamarind (lemon-sized ball) in a bowl
Add 250 ml warm water
Soak for 15 minutes at room temperature
VALIDATION: Tamarind becomes soft and pliable. Water turns light brown.
Cut 170 g tender drumsticks into 2-inch uniform pieces
Slice 150 g onion into thin, even slices (¼ inch)
Dice 100 g tomato into small bite-sized pieces
Grate 10 g fresh ginger finely (about 1 tbsp)
Finely chop 20 g coriander leaves with tender stems (about ½ cup)
Measure out curry leaves (15-20 fresh leaves)
VALIDATION: Drumstick pieces are uniform. Onion slices are thin and translucent. Ginger is finely grated. Coriander stems are tender.
Wash 120 g toor dal under running water 3 times until water runs clear
Add washed dal to pressure cooker
Add 500 ml water
Add 1 g turmeric powder (¼ tsp)
Stir to combine
Pressure cook on high heat until 4 whistles
Allow pressure to release naturally
VALIDATION: Dal grains are fully cooked and soft. Dal mashes easily with back of spoon. No hard grains remain.

ingredients.name: Toor Dal
ingredients.notes: also called kandi pappu in Andhra

subSteps: Using hands or spoon, mash soaked tamarind thoroughly to extract juice
Strain the extract through a fine sieve into a clean bowl
Discard all fibers and seeds
Set tamarind extract aside
VALIDATION: Extract is smooth and pulp-free. No fibrous pieces visible. Dark brown color.
Heat a heavy-bottomed pot or kadai over medium heat
Add prepared drumstick pieces (2-inch cut)
Add sliced onion, diced tomato, grated ginger
Pour in tamarind extract and add salt (9 g)
Stir well to combine all ingredients
Cook uncovered over medium heat for 12-15 minutes
VALIDATION: Drumstick pieces become tender and easily pierced. Onion turns soft and translucent. Tomato starts breaking down. Liquid simmers gently.
Add 15 g sambar powder (1.5 tbsp) to the cooking sambar
Mix thoroughly with a spoon to dissolve completely
Cook for 2-3 minutes on medium heat
VALIDATION: Raw spice smell completely disappears. Sambar develops rich, warm Andhra aroma. Color becomes darker and deeper.
Take cooked dal from pressure cooker
Mash thoroughly with back of spoon until mostly smooth (some texture is okay)
Add mashed dal to the sambar pot
Pour 300-400 ml hot water into the pot
Stir thoroughly to distribute dal evenly
Reduce heat to low and simmer uncovered for 10 minutes
VALIDATION: Sambar becomes creamy and smooth. Drumstick flavor infuses into broth. Gentle bubbling visible on surface. Oil starts to appear.
In a small tadka pan, heat 10 g ghee + 10 g oil (2 tsp each) over medium-high heat
Once ghee is melted and oil is hot, add 3 g mustard seeds (½ tsp)
Allow seeds to sputter for 10-15 seconds, they will pop and splutter
Add 5 g chana dal (1 tsp) and 5 g urad dal (1 tsp)
Stir constantly for 2-3 minutes until dals become light golden
Add 2 g cumin seeds (½ tsp), stir for 10 seconds
Add thinly sliced garlic (1-2 cloves), reduce to medium heat
Cook garlic for 1-2 minutes until light golden (not brown)
Add 2 dry red chillies (whole pieces)
Add curry leaves (6-8 leaves)
Add 1 pinch hing (asafetida)
Cook for 15 seconds until fragrance intensifies
VALIDATION: Mustard seeds sputter and pop. Dals become golden. Garlic is light golden and aromatic. Dry chillies darken slightly. Strong, complex aroma develops throughout tadka.
Ensure sambar is still simmering gently on low heat
Carefully pour the hot tadka over the simmering sambar
The tadka will sizzle and create aromatic oil
Immediately add 20 g chopped coriander leaves and tender stems (½ cup)
Gently fold coriander into the sambar
Cover the pot immediately with a lid
Remove from heat and let rest for 10 minutes
Stir gently before serving
VALIDATION: Fresh coriander aroma spreads. Tadka fragrance permeates entire sambar. Oil droplets visible on surface. Sambar is creamy and well-blended. Flavors have mellowed and integrated.

seo.metaDescription: Learn to make authentic Andhra-style drumstick sambar with tender drumsticks, tamarind tang, and aromatic tadka. Perfect with rice and South Indian dishes.
